title: Ensure that the kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root

input_row.title: Ensure that the kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root
input_row.description: Ensure that the kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.rationale: The kubelet service file controls various parameters that set the behavior of the kubelet service in the worker node. You should set its file ownership to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be owned by root:root .
input_row.audit: Automated AAC auditing has been modified to allow CIS-CAT to input a variable for the <PATH>/<FILENAME> of the kubelet service config file. Please set $kubelet_service_config=<PATH> based on the file location on your system for example: export kubelet_service_config=/etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/kubeadm.conf To perform the audit manually: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on each worker node. For example, stat -c %a /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/10-kubeadm.conf Verify that the ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on each worker node. For example, chown root:root /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/kubeadm.conf
input_row.default_value: By default, kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into each worker node where the kubelet service is running.
2. Run the command: `stat -c %U:%G /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/kubeadm.conf` (or the appropriate path to the kubelet service file on your system).
3. Verify that the output shows `root:root`.
4. If the ownership is not set to `root:root`, run the command: `chown root:root /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/kubeadm.conf` to correct it.
